text:
1. Table Overview

Table Name: africa_soil_moisture

What the Table Entails

This table contains daily volumetric soil moisture measurements for the African continent, retrieved from the European Space Agency (ESA) Climate Change Initiative (CCI). It models the physical moisture content in the topsoil layer (0–5 cm depth).

Type: Time-series Geospatial Grid.

Granularity: One row per unique coordinate (Latitude/Longitude) per day.

Purpose: Serves as the raw "Bronze" layer for environmental monitoring and agricultural analysis in Africa.

2. Source Details (FTP/Scientific Data Specific)

Data Provider: ESA CCI (Climate Change Initiative) via CEDA Archive.

Access Method: FTP (File Transfer Protocol).

Source Format: NetCDF4 (Binary Scientific Data).

Path Structure: /neodc/esacci/soil_moisture/data/daily_files/COMBINED/{version}/{year}/

Authentication: Anonymous FTP Login.

Pagination Strategy: File-based iteration (one file per day).

/{version}/{year}/

Authentication: Anonymous FTP Login.

Pagination Strategy: File-based iteration (one file per day).

Rate Limits: No formal API limit, but subject to standard FTP connection stability.

Ingestion Frequency: Batch (On-demand or Daily).

Historical Coverage: From 1978 to 2025 (depending on version availability).

3. Relationships & Dependencies

Primary Key: Composite Key of (lat, lon, time).

Foreign Keys: None (This is a base observational table).

Relationship Type: One-to-Many (One geographic coordinate has many daily moisture readings).

Join Notes: Best joined with a Geography Master Table on lat and lon to map coordinates to specific countries, districts, or farm boundaries.

4. Column-Level Documentation

Column Name: lat

Description: The north-south geographic coordinate.

Source Field: lat (NetCDF Dimension).

Data Type: FLOAT64.

Format: Decimal Degrees (WGS84).

Nullable: No.

Example Value: 5.235

Relationship: Part of Composite Primary Key.

4.

Format: Decimal Degrees (WGS84).

Nullable: No.

Example Value: 5.235

Relationship: Part of Composite Primary Key.

Transformation Logic: Sliced during ingestion to include only the range $38$ to $-35$.

Column Name: lon

Description: The east-west geographic coordinate.

Source Field: lon (NetCDF Dimension).

Data Type: FLOAT64.

Format: Decimal Degrees (WGS84).

Nullable: No.

Example Value: 21.450

Relationship: Part of Composite Primary Key.

Transformation Logic: Sliced during ingestion to include only the range $-20$ to $52$.

Column Name: time

Description: The specific day the measurement was recorded.

Source Field: time (NetCDF Dimension).

Data Type: TIMESTAMP.

Format: ISO-8601 UTC.

Nullable: No.

Example Value: 2022-01-01T00:00:00Z

Relationship: Part of Composite Primary Key.

Transformation Logic: Converted from NetCDF "days since" format to standard Timestamp.

Column Name: sm

Description: Volumetric Soil Moisture (water content in the soil).

nce" format to standard Timestamp.

Column Name: sm

Description: Volumetric Soil Moisture (water content in the soil).

Source Field: sm (Data Variable).

Data Type: FLOAT64.

Format: $m^3/m^3$ (cubic meters of water per cubic meter of soil).

Nullable: No (NaN values are dropped during ingestion).

Example Value: 0.2458

Relationship: Observed Value.

Transformation Logic: Extracted from the 3D grid and flattened into a table row.

Notes: Missing values (usually over oceans or ice) are filtered out before the data reaches the Bronze table to save storage.

1. Table Overview

Table Name

Name: fews_net_food_security_master_bronze

What the Table Entails

This table is the "Bronze" long-term repository for food security classifications provided by the Famine Early Warning Systems Network (FEWS NET). It tracks how food security levels change over time across different regions.

Real-world Entity: It represents regional and national food security assessments based on the IPC (Integrated Food Security Phase Classification) scale.

Type: Time-series data.

Granularity: One row per geographic unit-scenario-projection period.

2. Source Details

API/Source Provider: FEWS NET (Data Warehouse)

API Type: REST API / Bulk CSV Export

Update Frequency: Monthly (Outlook Briefs) and Quarterly (Full Outlooks).

Historical Coverage: Varies by country; generally 2009 to 2026.

3. Relationships & Dependencies

Primary Key: Composite Key (country_code, geographic_unit_name, projection_start, scenario_name).

Relationship Type:

Primary Key: Composite Key (country_code, geographic_unit_name, projection_start, scenario_name).

Relationship Type:

Temporal: Linked to the iSDA soil data via the country or latitude/longitude centroids for environmental-impact analysis.

Join Notes: When joining with spatial data, use geographic_unit_name for admin-level mapping.

4. Column-Level Documentation

Column Name

Description

Data Type

Format / Example

country

Full name of the country.

STRING

Afghanistan, Ethiopia

country_code

ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code.

STRING

AF, ET

geographic_unit_name

The specific area being assessed.

STRING

Kabul, Central Highlands

unit_type

Admin level (admin0 = National, admin1 = State/Prov).

STRING

admin0

ipc_phase_value

The numeric IPC Phase score.

FLOAT64

3.0 (Phase 3)

ipc_description

The classification label.

STRING

Crisis, Emergency

scenario_name

Forecast type (Most Likely, Pessimistic).

STRING

Most Likely

projection_start

Start of the forecast period.

DATE

Forecast type (Most Likely, Pessimistic).

STRING

Most Likely

projection_start

Start of the forecast period.

DATE

2025-05-01

projection_end

End of the forecast period.

DATE

2025-05-31

reporting_date

Date the data was officially recorded.

DATE

2024-11-01

created_at

Metadata: When the record hit the FEWS system.

TIMESTAMP

2024-11-14 19:50:28

ingested_at

Metadata: When the row moved to Bronze.

TIMESTAMP

2026-01-31 16:30:00

5. Understanding the Values (IPC Scale)

The ipc_phase_value column follows the international standard for food insecurity.

Value

Phase Name

Description

1.0

Minimal

Households are able to meet essential food and non-food needs.

2.0

Stressed

Food consumption is minimally adequate; unable to afford some essential non-food items.

3.0

Crisis

High or above usual acute malnutrition; households have food consumption gaps.

4.0

Emergency

Very high acute malnutrition and excess mortality; extreme loss of livelihood assets.

5.0

Famine

ps.

4.0

Emergency

Very high acute malnutrition and excess mortality; extreme loss of livelihood assets.

5.0

Famine

Extreme lack of food; starvation, death, and destitution are evident.

Table Overview

Source = OpenAIRE - Research publications and linked data from agriculture and environment

openaire_data_sources_raw

1. Table Name Name: openaire_data_sources_raw

serves as the landing (EL) layer for data source information retrieved from the OpenAIRE Graph API. It acts as a raw ingestion log, capturing the full JSON response from the provider.

Entity/Event: Represents a batch of OpenAIRE data sources (repositories, journals, etc.) fetched during an ingestion run.

Type: Event Log / Ingestion Snapshot.

Granularity: One row per API request/response cycle. Each row contains a "page" of multiple results within the payload field.

2. Source Details (Web API Specific)

API Provider: OpenAIRE (Graph API)

API Type: REST

Endpoint(s): https://api.openaire.eu/graph/v1/dataSources

HTTP Method: GET

Authentication: None / Public

Pagination Strategy: Cursor-based (as indicated by nextCursor in the payload)

Rate Limits: free

Ingestion Frequency: Scheduled (Batch)

gy: Cursor-based (as indicated by nextCursor in the payload)

Rate Limits: free

Ingestion Frequency: Scheduled (Batch)

Historical Coverage: Full catalog of OpenAIRE-registered data sources.

3. Relationships & Dependencies

Primary Key: N/A

Foreign Keys: N/A (Raw layer).

Relationship Type:

One-to-Many: One row in this table contains multiple data source entities inside the payload JSON.

Join Notes: This table is not joined in its raw state.

4. Column-Level Documentation

Column: ingestion_id

Description: Unique identifier for the ingestion record, typically a hash of the content or a UUID.

Source Field: Generated during ingestion (Python hashlib).

Data Type: STRING

Format: MD5/Hex String

Nullable: No

Example Value: e0d096e369515fb7cde2e1865edb87d6

Relationship: N/A

Column: source

Description: The origin system of the data.

Source Field: 'OPENAIRE'

Data Type: STRING

Format: Uppercase string

Nullable: No

Example Value: OPENAIRE

Column: dataset

Field: 'OPENAIRE'

Data Type: STRING

Format: Uppercase string

Nullable: No

Example Value: OPENAIRE

Column: dataset

Description: The specific entity type or collection being fetched.

Source Field: 'dataSources'

Data Type: STRING

Format: camelCase

Nullable: No

Example Value: dataSources

Column: endpoint

Description: The full URL path where the data was requested.

Source Field: Request URL

Data Type: STRING

Format: URL

Nullable: No

Example Value: https://api.openaire.eu/graph/v1/dataSources

Column: page_offset

Description: The offset or page number used during the API call.

Source Field: Query parameter (if applicable)

Data Type: INTEGER

Format: Numeric

Nullable: Yes

Example Value: null (if using cursor-based pagination)

Column: http_status

Description: The HTTP response code returned by the API.

Source Field: response.status_code

Data Type: INTEGER

Format: 3-digit integer

Nullable: No

Example Value: 200

Column: fetched_at

response.status_code

Data Type: INTEGER

Format: 3-digit integer

Nullable: No

Example Value: 200

Column: fetched_at

Description: Timestamp indicating when the API call was made.

Source Field: datetime.now()

Data Type: TIMESTAMP

Format: ISO-8601 UTC

Nullable: No

Example Value: 2026-01-16 08:20:12 UTC

Column: payload

Description: The full raw JSON string returned by the OpenAIRE API.

Source Field: response.text

Data Type: STRING (JSON)

Format: JSON String

Nullable: No

Example Value: {"header":{...}, "results":[...]}

Notes: Contains nested metadata like numFound and the actual results array.

Column: status

Description: The outcome of the ingestion script processing.

Source Field: Internal script status

Data Type: STRING

Format: Lowercase text

Nullable: No

Example Value: success

openaire_product_links_raw

1. Table Name Name: openaire_product_links_raw

text

Nullable: No

Example Value: success

openaire_product_links_raw

1. Table Name Name: openaire_product_links_raw

Stores raw research product data (publications, datasets, and software) harvested from the OpenAIRE Graph API..

Entity/Event: Represents a batch of research products associated with a specific type (e.g., all publications).

Type: Event Log / Batch Ingestion Snapshot.

Granularity: One row per API request. Each row contains a "page" of up to 100 research products within the payload JSON.

2. Source Details (Web API Specific)

API Provider: OpenAIRE (Graph API)

API Type: REST

Endpoint(s): https://api.openaire.eu/graph/v1/researchProducts

HTTP Method: GET

Authentication: None / Public

Pagination Strategy: Cursor-based (using the cursor parameter and nextCursor from headers).

Rate Limits: free

Ingestion Frequency: Periodic/Scheduled Bulk Harvest.

Historical Coverage: Full OpenAIRE index for publications, datasets, and software.

3.

Periodic/Scheduled Bulk Harvest.

Historical Coverage: Full OpenAIRE index for publications, datasets, and software.

3. Relationships & Dependencies

Primary Key: N/A

Foreign Keys: N/A (Raw Layer).

Relationship Type:

One-to-Many: One row (one ingestion event) contains a collection of multiple research products in the payload.

Join Notes: This table is not joined in its raw state.

4. Column-Level Documentation

Column: ingestion_id

Description: Unique identifier for the specific API request/page fetched.

Source Field: Generated using MD5 hash of PROD|{type}|{cursor}.

Data Type: STRING

Format: MD5 Hex String

Nullable: No

Example Value: 329ruvESsCUBuEUEogy4mHLo2DmbgTKAe8

Relationship: N/A

Column: source_pair

Description: The specific product type category being requested.

Source Field: Defined in the script QUERIES list (e.g., publication, dataset).

Data Type: STRING

Format: Lowercase (Enums: publication, dataset, software)

Nullable: Yes

Example Value: publication

Data Type: STRING

Format: Lowercase (Enums: publication, dataset, software)

Nullable: Yes

Example Value: publication

Column: payload

Description: The full, unparsed JSON response from the OpenAIRE API.

Source Field: resp.text

Data Type: STRING (JSON)

Format: JSON String

Nullable: No

Example Value: {"header":{"numFound":214088461...}, "results":[...]}

Notes: Contains the results array which holds the actual metadata for each research product.

Column: fetched_at

Description: Timestamp (UTC) indicating when the data was retrieved from the API.

Source Field: datetime.now(timezone.utc)

Data Type: TIMESTAMP

Format: ISO-8601 UTC

Nullable: No

Example Value: 2026-01-22 08:31:21 UTC

Column: status

Description: Indicates the success or failure of the data ingestion for that specific page.

Source Field: Hardcoded as 'success' upon successful response

Data Type: STRING

Format: Lowercase string

Nullable: No

ource Field: Hardcoded as 'success' upon successful response

Data Type: STRING

Format: Lowercase string

Nullable: No

Example Value: success
